data.narrative:
Atmos Space Cargo is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Sebastian Klaus, Marta Oliveira, Jeffrey Hendrikse, and Christian Grimm, having raised approximately $14M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 public space & aerospace names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs.

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $30M total raised proxy and applying a 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, yields $60M. Peer-median triangulation across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B)—produces a median of $400M, with a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable, as the target's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M (low), $230M (median), and $460M (high). The substantial spread reflects tension between stage-based progression ($60M) and peer comparables ($400M median). Key uncertainties include the specific technology differentiation relative to the four peer references, near-term revenue trajectory, and whether the company's current stage justification warrants positioning toward the ClearSpace comparable ($400M Series A) or the lower stage-progression bound.
